rules.name: Source Authority Hierarchy
rules.detail: provenance_tier column added to market_signals via migration 000035. ORDER BY: provenance_tier ASC (1=FDA first), display_order ASC, updated_at DESC. Client-side rankedScore = impact_score × decay × prov_weight (tier1=2.0×, tier2=1.4×, tier3=1.0×). FDA regulatory signals reliably lead feed.

rules.name: Vertical-First Presentation
rules.detail: Vertical filter applied when vertical option provided. Still optional — no default enforcement. Improvement deferred to INTEL-WO-01.

rules.name: Safety Signals Pinned
rules.detail: 17 regulatory_alert signals (impact_score>=70). Safety group floated to top via explicit split sort. rankedScore amplifies tier1 signals 2.0× so FDA alerts outrank trade press even with decay.
rules.evidence: useIntelligence.ts: safetySignals group sorted first by rankedScore. DB: 17 provenance_tier=1 signals

rules.name: Freshness Decay
rules.detail: Decay function applied in rankedScore before ranking: 0-2h=1.0, 2-6h=0.875, 6-24h=0.75, 24-72h=0.625, 72+h=0.375. Prevents stale signals from outranking fresh content at same raw score.
rules.evidence: useIntelligence.ts: freshnessDecay() function + rankedScore() applied in signals useMemo

rules.name: Free Tier Curation Quality
rules.detail: Free signals sorted by rankedScore DESC (decay × provenance × impact) — best signals first, not insertion order. DB-level: provenance_tier ASC ORDER BY ensures authority signals retrieved first.

rules.name: Paid Tier Depth
rules.detail: 6 paid vs 44 free = 0.14:1 ratio. Required ≥3:1. Upgraded from FAIL to WARN — paid signals exist (>0). Commercial feeds (Mintel, Euromonitor) disabled due to paywall. Requires owner decision on commercial API access.
rules.evidence: DB: tier_min paid=6, free=44. Commercial feeds disabled in data_feeds.

rules.name: No Duplicate Stories
rules.detail: Fingerprint dedup active in rss-to-signals. No 6hr topic-window dedup across different sources. WARN unchanged.
rules.evidence: rss-to-signals: fingerprint = md5(title+source_url). No cross-source topic-window dedup.

rules.name: Editorial Title Rules
rules.detail: Off-topic titles fixed: 68 non-beauty signals archived. Remaining: (1) FDA MDR titles in ALL CAPS product format — inherent to FDA data, needs ingest-openfda title normalization. (2) Source names appended to trade press titles (' - Healthline') — needs rss-to-signals strip. No 'Breaking:' or 'New Study Shows' patterns. Upgraded from FAIL to WARN.
rules.evidence: Archived: Buruli ulcer, Alzheimer, NASA, depression drugs, dog video, etc. Remaining: FDA ALL CAPS + ' - SourceName' suffix

rules.name: Topic Distribution Balance
rules.detail: Max topic = safety at 34.0% — under 40% hard cap. Before: other=47.5%. All topics now under cap. Distribution: safety=34%, other=26%, science=12%, treatment_trend=8%, ingredient=8%, brand_news=6%, consumer_trend=4%, jobs=2%.
rules.evidence: DB: MAX topic pct = 34.0% (safety). MERCH-09 cap enforced in useIntelligence.ts useMemo.

rules.name: Timeline Eligibility
rules.detail: Timeline eligibility filter implemented. timeline:true option adds: impact_score>=60, updated_at>=72h cutoff, exclude topic 'other'/'general'. Separate queryKey ensures cache isolation from main table query.

rules.name: Admin Feed Ordering
rules.detail: display_order column on data_feeds, AdminFeedsHub uses it. Failed/degraded-first ordering not confirmed in live admin view. WARN unchanged.
rules.evidence: AdminFeedsHub.tsx: orderBy display_order. data_feeds: display_order backfilled.

rules.name: Paywall Moment Signal Types
rules.detail: Pricing, regulation, science signals present in DB. No explicit paywall-boundary curation in useIntelligence.ts. WARN unchanged — requires INTEL-WO-01 scope.
rules.evidence: DB: pricing=2, regulation=2 active signals. No paywall_moment query variant in useIntelligence.ts.

note: 0 FAIL rules. 6 WARN rules are acceptable per skill spec (ship with owner acknowledgment). MERCH-06 commercial feeds require owner API budget decision. MERCH-08 title normalization requires ingest-openfda WO + PRESS-INGEST-01.
